name: Steamed Fish and Vegetables in Stove-Top Papillotte
recipeIngredient: 1 cup sugar snap peas, blanched for 10 seconds
2 boneless pieces of halibut (6 to 8 ounces each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Tear off two large pieces of plastic wrap designed for microwave use; each should be large enough to enclose the fish and vegetables in a single layer.
2. Rub lemon juice on both sides of the halibut.
3. Layer half a cup of snow peas at the bottom of one piece of plastic wrap, drizzling with a teaspoon of olive oil. Place the fish over that and bring up the plastic wrap to totally enclose the fish.
4. Set a cake rack over simmering water in a deep skillet. Turn the fish packages upside down so the snow peas are on top. Layer them on the cake rack and cover with a round bowl or cover to entrain steam.
5. Cook for 5 minutes, or until the fish and vegetables are cooked through.
6. While the fish is steaming, remove tomato seeds with a spoon and cut into fine dice; toss with a bit of olive oil, salt, and pepper, then spoon onto dinner plates.
7. When the fish is done, carefully open the package using scissors (watch out for your hands). Place the fish and vegetables over the tomatoes and drizzle with juices. Season with salt and pepper and garnish with basil.
Yield: 2 servings
